title: NOC 22221 – User Support Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: User Support Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, driven by digital transformation, cloud adoption, cybersecurity needs, and the expansion of IT operations in government agencies, healthcare systems, financial institutions, and private enterprises. Classified under NOC 22221 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Communicate with users to diagnose technical issues
Research solutions using manuals and documentation
Train users on systems and applications
employment_requirements: College diploma in computer science, IT support, or network administration
Vendor certifications (Microsoft, CompTIA, ITIL, Cisco) preferred by many employers
job_outlook: This balanced but opportunity-rich outlook favours candidates with:
pathways: Career advancement into systems administration, cybersecurity, or IT management
additional_information: Canadian employers continue to recruit user-support professionals for:

IT help-desk & service centresSoftware-development firmsHardware manufacturers & retailersHealthcare & education networksFederal, provincial & municipal governmentsFinancial-services institutionsManaged IT service providers
Annual salaries commonly range from $39,000–$52,153 CAD, depending on province, certifications, and experience level.
